D12 PsychologyL2 UnderstandReference

Grapheme-Colour Synesthesia — A Is Red

~1% of people automatically perceive specific letters/numbers as specific colours; A trends red, O white/yellow, frequency-letter biases shared across populations

Grapheme-colour synesthesia is the most common form (~1.4%). Studies (Witthoft 2013) reveal colour-letter mappings often trace to childhood Fisher-Price magnet sets — same colour scheme appears in synesthetes who used them. Consistent within an individual across decades.

Drag letter cell to see typical synesthete colour.
